title: Putting feelings into words: A systematic review and meta-analysis of affect labeling
description:
An open-research package for a systematic review and random-effects meta-analysis of affect labeling — the psychological and neuroscientific phenomenon, originating in Lieberman et al. (2007), in which putting feelings into words attenuates emotional responses.

Pre-registration status. A PROSPERO-compatible structured pre-registration is included in the repository (prereg/PROSPERO_preregistration.md) but was not formally submitted to the PROSPERO registry. Screening was conducted by a single reviewer (deviation from the pre-registered two-reviewer protocol; inter-rater κ therefore unavailable; documented in §4.6 of the manuscript and in data/screening/README.md).

Headline result. Pooled Hedges' g = −0.43 (95% CI [−0.68, −0.18]) for peripheral physiology across 9 effect sizes from 7 independent studies. Lab-stratified analysis: g = −0.74 within the UCLA Lieberman/Craske axis, g = −0.13 in independent labs.

Contents. Full ~14,000-word manuscript, structured pre-registration document, PRISMA 2020 flow with structured per-stage counts, RoB 2 / ROBINS-I assessments, effect-size dataset, the derived screening corpus reconstructed from the canonical PubMed query, and Python code for byte-for-byte reproducibility of every numerical result and figure. The dataset is suitable for training and benchmarking AI-assisted SLR screening systems.
